name: Shuttle (Rust)
pricing_notes: Free tier: 3 projects, 500MB RAM; Pro $20/mo; Growth $50/mo

auth.notes: shuttle login (GitHub OAuth); project API keys for programmatic access

performance.notes: Rust performance; coldstart under 100ms; persistent containers (not true serverless)

agent_readiness.notes: Rust-native cloud deployment via annotation macros. `#[shuttle_runtime::main]` + `cargo shuttle deploy`. Auto-provisions databases, secrets, storage. No Dockerfile needed. Supports Axum, Actix-web, Rocket, Poem. CLI-first workflow.

gotchas: Rust-only — not applicable for non-Rust teams
Platform reliability less proven than AWS/GCP
Tightly coupled to Shuttle infrastructure (vendor lock-in)
best_when: Rust teams wanting zero-config deployment of Axum/Actix-web services
Deploying high-performance backend services without DevOps overhead
Building MCP servers in Rust that need simple hosting
Prototype-to-production in Rust with minimal infrastructure work
avoid_when: Enterprise production requiring proven platform reliability
Need multi-region or complex infrastructure setups
summary: Shuttle is a Rust-native PaaS where you deploy via annotation macros and `cargo shuttle deploy` — no Dockerfile, no Kubernetes, no DevOps. Supports major Rust web frameworks (Axum, Actix-web, Rocket). Auto-provisions resources declared in your code. Best for Rust teams who want Heroku-like simplicity for deploying high-performance backend services.
